drugs.name: Metformin
sideEffects: Vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use

interactions.description: Increases risk of lactic acidosis, a rare but life-threatening condition.
NSAIDs may impair renal function, reducing Metformin clearance.
ACE inhibitors may enhance the hypoglycemic effect. Monitor blood glucose.
Must be discontinued 48 hours before and after iodinated contrast procedures.

warnings: Contraindicated in severe renal impairment (eGFR < 30)
Risk of lactic acidosis in dehydration
Hold before surgical procedures
foodInteractions: Alcohol increases lactic acidosis risk
Take with food to reduce GI side effects

drugs.name: Lisinopril

interactions.description: Can cause dangerous hyperkalemia (elevated potassium).
NSAIDs reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk.
May enhance hypoglycemic effect. Monitor blood glucose.
Both increase potassium levels. High risk of hyperkalemia.

warnings: BLACK BOX: Do not use during pregnancy — causes fetal harm
Risk of angioedema — higher in Black patients
Monitor renal function and potassium levels
foodInteractions: Avoid potassium-rich salt substitutes
Limit potassium-rich foods (bananas, oranges)

drugs.name: Atorvastatin

interactions.description: Inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism, increasing statin levels and myopathy risk.
Strong CYP3A4 inhibitor — increases rhabdomyolysis risk.
May increase INR and bleeding risk. Monitor closely.
Do not exceed atorvastatin 20mg when combined with amlodipine 10mg.

warnings: Monitor liver function tests before and during therapy
Discontinue if unexplained muscle pain occurs
Contraindicated in active liver disease
foodInteractions: Avoid large quantities of grapefruit juice
Can be taken with or without food

drugs.name: Amoxicillin

interactions.description: May increase INR and bleeding risk. Monitor anticoagulation.
Penicillins reduce renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity.
May decrease effectiveness of hormonal contraceptives.
Increased incidence of skin rash when combined.

warnings: Allergy check: Do NOT prescribe to penicillin-allergic patients
Complete full course to prevent antibiotic resistance
May cause Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea
foodInteractions: Can be taken with or without food
Avoid dairy within 2 hours for optimal absorption

drugs.name: Amlodipine

interactions.description: Do not exceed simvastatin 20mg. Risk of rhabdomyolysis.
Increases cyclosporine levels. Monitor closely.
Limit atorvastatin to 20mg daily when combined.
May increase amlodipine levels via CYP3A4 inhibition.

warnings: Do not discontinue abruptly — risk of rebound hypertension
Use caution in severe hepatic impairment
Ankle swelling is common but usually benign
foodInteractions: Avoid excessive grapefruit juice
Can be taken with or without food

drugs.name: Omeprazole

interactions.description: Omeprazole reduces the antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel. Use pantoprazole instead.
PPIs can increase methotrexate levels and toxicity.
Reduced gastric acid impairs iron absorption.
Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, increasing diazepam levels.

warnings: Long-term use linked to magnesium deficiency, bone fractures, and kidney disease
Taper off gradually to avoid rebound acid hypersecretion
Test for H. pylori before long-term use
foodInteractions: Take 30-60 minutes before meals for maximum efficacy
Avoid alcohol — worsens gastric symptoms

drugs.name: Warfarin

interactions.description: Dramatically increases bleeding risk. Avoid unless specifically directed.
NSAIDs increase bleeding risk and may cause GI hemorrhage.
Antibiotics can alter gut flora and increase INR.
Dual antiplatelet + anticoagulant dramatically increases bleeding risk.

dosageInfo.frequency: Once daily at the same time

warnings: BLACK BOX: Can cause major or fatal bleeding
Requires regular INR monitoring
Numerous drug and food interactions — verify every new medication
foodInteractions: Maintain consistent Vitamin K intake (green leafy vegetables)
Avoid cranberry juice — may increase INR
Alcohol increases bleeding risk

drugs.name: Clopidogrel

interactions.description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing clopidogrel activation. Use pantoprazole instead.
Combined use dramatically increases bleeding risk.
Often prescribed together post-stent, but increases bleeding risk. Monitor closely.
SSRIs impair platelet function. Additive bleeding risk.

warnings: BLACK BOX: CYP2C19 poor metabolizers have reduced drug effectiveness
Discontinue 5-7 days before elective surgery
Consider genetic testing for CYP2C19 status
foodInteractions: Can be taken with or without food
No significant food interactions

drugs.name: Ibuprofen

interactions.description: Increases bleeding risk significantly. Avoid combination.
Reduces antihypertensive effect and increases renal impairment risk.
Ibuprofen may interfere with aspirin's cardioprotective effect.
May reduce renal function, impairing Metformin clearance.

warnings: BLACK BOX: Increased risk of cardiovascular thrombotic events
BLACK BOX: Increased risk of GI bleeding, ulceration, and perforation
Use lowest effective dose for shortest duration
foodInteractions: Take with food or milk to reduce stomach irritation
Avoid alcohol — increases GI bleeding risk

drugs.name: Aspirin

interactions.description: Dramatically increases bleeding risk. Monitor INR closely.
Ibuprofen may reduce aspirin's cardioprotective effect if taken before aspirin.
Dual antiplatelet increases bleeding risk but may be indicated post-stent.
Aspirin reduces renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity.

warnings: Do NOT give to children under 18 — Reye's syndrome risk
Discontinue 7-10 days before surgery
Use enteric-coated to reduce GI effects
foodInteractions: Take with food to reduce GI irritation
Avoid alcohol — increases GI bleeding risk

drugs.name: Levothyroxine

interactions.description: Calcium binds levothyroxine in the gut, reducing absorption. Separate by 4 hours.
Iron reduces absorption. Separate by 4 hours.
PPIs reduce gastric acid needed for levothyroxine absorption.
Levothyroxine increases warfarin's anticoagulant effect. Monitor INR.

dosageInfo.frequency: Once daily on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast

warnings: Take on empty stomach 30-60 minutes before food
Do not switch brands without physician approval — bioavailability differs
Overtreatment causes atrial fibrillation and bone loss
foodInteractions: Coffee, fiber, and soy reduce absorption — take separately
Avoid taking with breakfast — take 30-60 min before

drugs.name: Prednisone

interactions.description: Combined NSAID + corticosteroid dramatically increases GI ulcer risk.
Corticosteroids can alter INR in either direction. Monitor closely.
Prednisone raises blood glucose, counteracting Metformin's effect.
Immunosuppressive doses contraindicate live vaccines.

warnings: Do NOT stop abruptly after >7 days — taper gradually to avoid adrenal crisis
Increases infection risk at immunosuppressive doses
Monitor blood glucose in diabetic patients
foodInteractions: Take with food to reduce stomach irritation
High-sodium diet may worsen fluid retention

drugs.name: Losartan

interactions.description: Can cause dangerous hyperkalemia. Monitor potassium levels.
Do not combine ACE inhibitor + ARB — increased renal failure and hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk.
ARBs reduce lithium clearance, increasing toxicity risk.

warnings: BLACK BOX: Do not use during pregnancy — causes fetal harm
Monitor renal function and potassium levels
Do not combine with ACE inhibitors
foodInteractions: Can be taken with or without food
Avoid potassium-rich salt substitutes

drugs.name: Hydrochlorothiazide

interactions.description: Often used together intentionally. Monitor for excessive hypotension.
Thiazides reduce lithium clearance, increasing toxicity risk.
Hypokalemia from HCTZ increases digoxin toxicity risk.
HCTZ may raise blood glucose, counteracting Metformin.

warnings: Monitor electrolytes (potassium, sodium, magnesium)
May worsen gout by increasing uric acid
Increases photosensitivity — use sunscreen
foodInteractions: Take in the morning to avoid nighttime urination
Eat potassium-rich foods (bananas, potatoes) to prevent hypokalemia

drugs.name: Sertraline

interactions.description: CONTRAINDICATED — risk of fatal serotonin syndrome. 14-day washout required.
Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures.
SSRIs impair platelet function, increasing bleeding risk.
Additive effect on platelet function. Monitor for bleeding.

warnings: BLACK BOX: Increased suicidal thinking in children and young adults (under 25)
Do not discontinue abruptly — taper over 2-4 weeks
Monitor for serotonin syndrome symptoms
foodInteractions: Can be taken with or without food
Avoid alcohol — worsens depression and sedation

drugs.name: Gabapentin

interactions.description: Combined use increases risk of respiratory depression and death.
Aluminum/magnesium antacids reduce gabapentin absorption by 20%. Separate by 2 hours.
Enhances CNS depression. Avoid combination.
Morphine increases gabapentin levels. Increased sedation risk.

warnings: Do NOT stop abruptly — taper over at least one week to prevent seizures
Dose adjustment required in renal impairment
May cause suicidal ideation — monitor mental health
foodInteractions: Can be taken with or without food
Avoid alcohol — increases sedation

drugs.name: Pantoprazole

interactions.description: PPIs can increase methotrexate levels and toxicity.
May alter INR. Monitor anticoagulation.
Reduced gastric acid impairs iron absorption.
Pantoprazole has minimal CYP2C19 inhibition — preferred PPI with clopidogrel.

warnings: Long-term use linked to magnesium deficiency and bone fractures
Preferred over omeprazole when used with clopidogrel
Taper off to avoid rebound acid
foodInteractions: Take 30-60 minutes before meals
Swallow tablets whole — do not crush or chew

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin

interactions.description: Aluminum, magnesium, calcium antacids bind ciprofloxacin. Separate by 2 hours.
Ciprofloxacin significantly increases INR and bleeding risk.
Ciprofloxacin inhibits theophylline metabolism, increasing toxicity risk.
Combined use dramatically increases tendon rupture risk.

warnings: BLACK BOX: Tendinitis and tendon rupture — especially in patients >60, on corticosteroids, or with kidney/heart/lung transplants
BLACK BOX: May worsen myasthenia gravis
Avoid in patients with QT prolongation
foodInteractions: Avoid dairy products and calcium-fortified foods within 2 hours of dose
Drink plenty of water to prevent crystalluria

drugs.name: Albuterol

interactions.description: Beta-blockers antagonize albuterol's bronchodilating effect. Avoid non-selective beta-blockers.
Albuterol may decrease digoxin levels.
Both can cause hypokalemia. Monitor potassium.
May potentiate cardiovascular effects of albuterol.

warnings: Using more than 2 days per week indicates poorly controlled asthma
Rinse mouth after use if using with inhaled corticosteroid
Seek medical attention if not relieving symptoms
foodInteractions: No significant food interactions
Avoid caffeine — may worsen tremor and tachycardia

drugs.name: Vitamin D3

interactions.description: Thiazides reduce calcium excretion, increasing hypercalcemia risk with vitamin D.
Vitamin D-induced hypercalcemia increases digoxin toxicity risk.
Orlistat reduces fat absorption, decreasing vitamin D uptake.
Bile acid sequestrants reduce vitamin D absorption.

warnings: Monitor calcium and 25-OH vitamin D levels in high-dose therapy
Excessive intake causes hypercalcemia
Patients with kidney disease need active form (calcitriol)
foodInteractions: Take with a fat-containing meal for best absorption
No significant restrictions
